name: Potential Leakage of Data via Email Attachment
notes:
The rule triggers when numerous emails containing attachments are sent to an external email address that indicates potential leakage.

Note: The condition "and NOT when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host" has been added because the rule "Email Sent to Potentially Hostile Host" will alert on any email sent to a suspicious address. If you wish to have this additional information, remove the filter from the rule.
conditions_text: when an event matches all of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: External Email Addresses
when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Content Information
when at least 100 events are seen with the same Event Name in 5 minutes
thresholds: when at least 100 events are seen with the same Event Name in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Potential Leakage of Data via Email Attachment on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when an event matches all of the following BB:DeviceDefinition: Mail
AND when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: External Email Addresses
AND NOT when an event matches any of the following BB:BehaviorDefinition: Potentially Hostile Email Host
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Content Information
AND when at least 100 events are seen with the same Event Name in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Potential Leakage of Data via Email Attachment
Event description: Detects when an internal email address sends several emails containing attachments to an email address that is outside of the organization
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Sender (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
